name: status
description: Show agent status

name: jq
description:
Run jq for JSON filtering and transformation.
Typical usage hints: extract fields (`.foo`), iterate arrays (`.items[]`), filter (`select(...)`), build new JSON (`{k: .v}`).
Provide all jq CLI arguments via --args, including filter and optional file paths.

args.description: jq CLI arguments string. Example: `-r '.items[] | .name' data.json`

stdin.description: JSON text piped to jq. Useful when no input file is available.

name: awk
description:
Run awk for pattern matching and column-based text processing.
Typical usage hints: field extraction (`{print $1}`), filtering (`$3 > 10`), delimiter control (`-F ','`).
Provide all awk CLI arguments via --args.

args.description: awk CLI arguments string. Example: `-F ',' '{print $1}' data.csv`

stdin.description: Input text piped to awk. Useful for inline processing.

name: sed
description:
Run sed for stream editing.
Typical usage hints: substitute (`s/old/new/g`), print ranges (`-n '10,20p'`), in-place edits (`-i`) when needed.
Provide all sed CLI arguments via --args.

args.description: sed CLI arguments string. Example: `-n '1,20p' README.md`

name: head
description:
Run head for quick preview of the beginning of files or streams.
Typical usage hints: first N lines (`-n 50`), first bytes (`-c 200`).
Provide all head CLI arguments via --args.

args.description: head CLI arguments string. Example: `-n 40 logs/app.log`

name: tokei
description:
Run tokei for code statistics (lines, blanks, comments, language breakdown).
Typical usage hints: target project paths, use `--sort` or `--output json` for structured output.
Provide all tokei CLI arguments via --args.

args.description: tokei CLI arguments string. Example: `--output json src tests`

stdin.description: Input text piped to tokei when applicable.

name: dust
description:
Run dust for disk-usage visualization.
Typical usage hints: inspect directory sizes, limit depth (`-d`), reverse sort (`-r`).
Provide all dust CLI arguments via --args.

stdin.description: Input text piped to dust when applicable.

name: w3m
description:
Run w3m in dump mode (`--dump`) for text extraction from web pages or HTML.
This wrapper always enforces `w3m --dump` and appends your --args.
Typical usage hints: pass URL directly, tune width with `-cols`, provide HTML via --stdin plus `-T text/html`.

stdin.description: HTML text piped to w3m. Pair with args like `-T text/html` when needed.

name: cat
description:
Run cat for file concatenation and raw text output.
Typical usage hints: read files in order, include line numbers (`-n`) when needed.
Provide all cat CLI arguments via --args.

args.description: cat CLI arguments string. Example: `-n README.md`

name: rg
description:
Run ripgrep (`rg`) for fast text search.
Typical usage hints: basic search (`pattern path`), file filtering (`-g`), line numbers (`-n`), context (`-C`).
Provide all rg CLI arguments via --args.

args.description: rg CLI arguments string. Example: `-n 'TODO|FIXME' src`

name: fd
description:
Run fd for fast file discovery by pattern.
Typical usage hints: search by name (`pattern path`), type filtering (`-t f`/`-t d`), extension (`-e`).
Provide all fd CLI arguments via --args.

args.description: fd CLI arguments string. Example: `-t f -e md README .`

stdin.description: Input text piped to fd when applicable.

name: ps_aux
description:
Show process list using `ps aux`.
Useful for checking high CPU or memory usage, process owners, and command lines.

filter.description: Filter regex to apply to `ps aux` output.

name: lsof_listen
description:
Show listening TCP sockets for a specific port using `lsof -nP -iTCP:<PORT> -sTCP:LISTEN`.
Use this to identify which process owns a port.

name: pwd
description:
Show current working directory using `pwd`.
Pass extra pwd options via --args when needed.

args.description: Optional pwd CLI arguments string. Example: `-P`

name: mkdir
description:
Create directories using `mkdir -p`.
This wrapper always includes `-p` so existing directories do not fail.

name: tail
description:
Run tail for viewing end of files or streams.
Typical usage hints: last N lines (`-n 100`), follow mode (`-f`).
Provide all tail CLI arguments via --args.

args.description: tail CLI arguments string. Example: `-n 50 app.log`

name: wc
description:
Run wc for counting lines, words, and bytes.
Typical usage hints: line count (`-l`), word count (`-w`), byte count (`-c`).
Provide all wc CLI arguments via --args.

args.description: wc CLI arguments string. Example: `-l src/main.rs`

name: sort_uniq
description:
Aggregate values with `sort | uniq`.
Typical usage hints: count duplicates (`uniq -c`), ignore case (`sort -f`, `uniq -i`).

name: xargs
description:
Run xargs for pipeline argument expansion.
Typical usage hints: build command calls from stdin, use `-I {}` for placeholders, use `-0` with null-delimited input.
Provide all xargs CLI arguments via --args.

args.description: xargs CLI arguments string. Example: `-I {} echo file:{}`

name: curl
description:
Run curl for lightweight HTTP checks.
Typical usage hints: headers only (`-I`), silent fetch (`-s`), method/body (`-X`, `-H`, `--data`).
Provide all curl CLI arguments via --args.

args.description: curl CLI arguments string. Example: `-s https://example.com`

stdin.description: Input text piped to curl, useful with `--data-binary @-`.
